rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MALEE TANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/19/2025, as the amended petition filed on 02/03/2025 did not address the following issues (initially e-mailed to counsel on 08/20/2024 - see also tentative rulings for 11/26/2024 & 12/31/2024 hearings): 1) No GC-020 form (notice of hearing with proof of service) is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form on the conservatee at least 15 days before the hearing. Note: The word "amended" must be inserted at item 1 of the GC-020 form, together with counsel's fees as additional relief sought, though the petition caption did not include the relief. See CRC 7.50 & 7.102. 2) An allegation required by Probate Code section 1064(a)(3) must be made to disclose any family or affiliate relationship between the petitioner and any agent hired by the petitioner during the accounting period. See also CRC 7.575(a)(3). 3) The summary of account must be filed on the mandatory Judicial Council form GC-400(SUM). See CRC 7.575(e)(5). 4) The corrected final inventory and appraisal filed on 02/03/2025 was not signed by the probate referee. 5) No supplemental inventory and appraisal has been filed and served for the UPS and OMC shares reported as additional property received (Schedule 1C). See section 2613. 5) No schedule of liabilities (see section 1063(g)) was included. The fees and costs of the former court-appointed attorney for the conservatee were approved by the Court on 07/20/2023, but the schedule of disbursements does not show payment.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BEATRIZ QUITOLES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/25. Examiner's Notes sent regarding the following issues: 1) Creditor's claim filed on 7/24/23: a. Decedent died in 2000. Please address whether the claim was timely filed and properly paid. b. The creditor's claim includes the time period after Decedent's death, which would generally not be a creditor's claim. Please categorize the amounts properly and sufficiently. 2) Deposits are listed in the accounting without any description. 3) Transactions in the accounting pertain to 100% interest in the real property, but the estate only has a 1/3 interest. The transactions must pertain only to the 1/3 interest. Accounting and request for fees will be reviewed once the accounting is amended. 4) The Notice of Proposed Action filed on 7/5/24 states that the real property will be sold for a total of $598K; however, the real property was sold for $520K. Consent to transaction will need to be filed by the beneficiary, Dexter Quitoles.

rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/25. The following issues must be cleared two weeks prior to the next hearing: (1) Petition must be verified by the fiduciary. Prob. Code sec. 1021. Re-notice required with the correct caption/title of the petition. (4) Notice of Directions for Remote appearance and Access must be given. LRSF 14.1.C. (5) Proposed order must be submitted. LRSF 14.7.A.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LARRY DENNIS MENKE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/25. Examiner's Notes emailed to attorney with the following issues: 1) A copy of the lost will must be filed in the same way as an original will is filed. 2) Item 3f(3) is marked but Attachment 3f(3) is incomplete as it does not include discussion of Probate Code 6124. Please read Item 3f(3) carefully. 3) Proposed order must be re-filed, filled out with the assumption that the petition will be granted as requested. A copy of the lost will must also be attached to the order. 4) Caption of the petition does not match the body. Please confirm that Petitioner is requesting full IAEA authority. 5) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestors through whom they take (or would have taken) must be named and their relationships to the decedent and dates of deaths need to be stated [Local Rule 14.18C]. 6) Item 6f is marked, but cousins (issue of grandparents) are listed in Item 8. Note: the first applicable box must be marked. 7) The trustee of the Larry Menke Trust of 2001 is not listed / identified. If Petitioner is the trustee, the beneficiaries of the trust must also be listed / identified and given notice, if not already given notice. 8) A copy of the Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License was attached to the Duties and Liabilities and is currently publicly viewable. Immediate steps should be taken to remove the filing with the filing clerk.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE MARRIAGE OF HYONG THI QUACH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION TO ESTABLISH FACT OF MARRIAGE
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Petition does not present sufficient supporting information or material for the court to establish the fact. Need more information to support the marriage. For example, when/where the ceremony took place in detail. Photos. Who performed the ceremony? A written declaration from the person who performed it. Witnesses' statements describing the ceremony regarding the marriage or ceremony. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.
